channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Swiss reject right-wing's bid to cap population at 10 million, early results show
item.description: Nearly 55% of voters in Switzerland rejected an initiative championed by the top right-wing party to cap the rich Alpine country's population at 10 million, early results showed.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 55% of voters in Switzerland rejected an initiative championed by the top right-wing party to cap the rich Alpine country's population at 10 million, early results showed.

(Image credit: Laurent Gillieron)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Russian attack sets fire to religious site in Kyiv, kills 5 in Kharkiv
item.description: A large-scale Russian attack killed 5 rescuers in Kharkiv, wounded 20 in the capital Kyiv, set apartment buildings ablaze and sparked a fire at one of Ukraine's most significant religious landmarks.
item.content:encoded:
A large-scale Russian attack killed 5 rescuers in Kharkiv, wounded 20 in the capital Kyiv, set apartment buildings ablaze and sparked a fire at one of Ukraine's most significant religious landmarks.

(Image credit: Evgeniy Maloletka)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Crude oil futures drop after Trump promises an Iran deal will be signed Friday
item.description: Oil prices had already fallen quite dramatically on Thursday and Friday, in anticipation of an imminent deal. President Trump has posted online that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen after the deal is signed on Friday.
item.content:encoded:
Oil prices had already fallen quite dramatically on Thursday and Friday, in anticipation of an imminent deal. President Trump has posted online that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen after the deal is signed on Friday.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: The U.S. and Iran announce a deal to end the war
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. would remove its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and said a deal will be signed on Friday.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said the U.S. would remove its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and said a deal will be signed on Friday.

(Image credit: Samuel Corum)
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: G7 leaders face wars abroad and divisions at home
item.description: President Trump and other G7 leaders meet in the French Alps this week as wars, economic uncertainty and strains in the transatlantic alliance loom over the summit.
item.content:encoded: President Trump and other G7 leaders meet in the French Alps this week as wars, economic uncertainty and strains in the transatlantic alliance loom over the summit.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: As Iranians wait for a peace agreement, their economy is fraying
item.description: Many Iranians say they can no longer afford the lives they once had. Writer and historian Arash Azizi talks with NPR's Adrian Florido about the economic pressures reshaping daily life.
item.content:encoded: Many Iranians say they can no longer afford the lives they once had. Writer and historian Arash Azizi talks with NPR's Adrian Florido about the economic pressures reshaping daily life.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: Britain detains sanctioned oil tanker believed to be linked to Russia's shadow fleet
item.description: Britain is investigating a sanctioned tanker that is suspected of being part of the Russian "shadow fleet," shipping oil in violation of international sanctions over Moscow's war on Ukraine.
item.content:encoded:
Britain is investigating a sanctioned tanker that is suspected of being part of the Russian "shadow fleet," shipping oil in violation of international sanctions over Moscow's war on Ukraine.

(Image credit: Kin Cheung)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump condemns Israeli strike in Beirut, warning attacks threaten deal on U.S-Iran war
item.description: Trump said Israel's attack on Beirut Sunday "should not have happened," but maintained that the U.S. was still "very close to a Deal" to end the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
Trump said Israel's attack on Beirut Sunday "should not have happened," but maintained that the U.S. was still "very close to a Deal" to end the war with Iran.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat)
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman

item.title: World Cup online betting takes a toll in Brazil
item.description: A World Cup fever is sweeping Brazil. So is sports betting. NPR's Julia Carneiro reports on the country's gambling boom and the growing concerns about debt and addiction.
item.content:encoded: A World Cup fever is sweeping Brazil. So is sports betting. NPR's Julia Carneiro reports on the country's gambling boom and the growing concerns about debt and addiction.
item.dc:creator: Júlia Dias Carneiro

item.title: A Lebanon town's grief in the aftermath of a deadly Israeli airstrike
item.description: More than 3,700 people in Lebanon have died in the war between Israel and Hezbollah. In a village in southern Lebanon, one airstrike last month killed 14 people, including 10 women and children.
item.content:encoded: More than 3,700 people in Lebanon have died in the war between Israel and Hezbollah. In a village in southern Lebanon, one airstrike last month killed 14 people, including 10 women and children.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf
